prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The North American winter of 2014-15 was characterized by low temperatures and a high chance of precipitation, particularly in the eastern half of North America. The season started early, with many places in North America experiencing their first wintry weather in mid-November. A period of below-average temperatures affected much of the contiguous United States, and several records were broken. Additionally, there was a greater amount of snowfall across parts of Oklahoma, and a quasi-permanent phenomenon referred to as the polar vortex. This may have caused temperatures to drop from mid-April to mid-late
B. The winter season of 2014–15 in North America was characterized by intensely cold and exceptionally snowy conditions, particularly throughout the eastern portion of the continent during the months of January, February, and March. The cold weather commenced sooner than usual, with numerous locations across North America encountering their initial winter weather events as early as mid-November. A sustained period of temperatures below the seasonal average impacted a significant portion of the contiguous United States, leading to the establishment of several new record lows. Even Arkansas experienced an initial, light dusting of snow. Furthermore, certain areas of Oklahoma received more substantial snowfall accumulations. A persistent atmospheric occurrence, known as the polar vortex, is believed to have contributed in part to these frigid temperatures. By November 19, temperatures in a large part of the United States had plummeted between 15 and 35 °F (equivalent to 8.3 to 19 °C) below the typical average, a change that followed a southward "descent" of the polar vortex into the eastern part of the continent.
C. The 2014–15 North American winter was frigid and prolifically wintry, especially across the eastern half of North America in the months of January–March. The season began early, with many places in North America experiencing their first wintry weather during mid-November. A period of below-average temperatures affected much of the contiguous United States, and several records were broken. An early trace of snowfall was recorded in Arkansas. There were greater accumulations of snow across parts of Oklahoma as well. A quasi-permanent phenomenon referred to as the polar vortex may have been partly responsible for the cold weather. Temperatures in much of the United States dropped 15 to 35 °F (8.3 to 19 °C) below average by November 19, following a southward "dip" of the polar vortex into the eastern

Answer:
